access.guest_fallback_message: Detailed explanations are available for logged-in Saola students only.

q14.explanation: Cần điền/chọn thông tin đúng từ bài đọc. Ở đoạn 2, bài viết nêu: "You might be surprised to learn that time as we know it - a precise measurement structured into ordered geographic zones of regular intervals - is a relatively recent phenomenon. If you went back as few as two or three centuries, you would find the human experience of time to be quite different, especially if your time travel was followed by travel of the traditional variety. For example, whereas now London and Bristol - approximately 100 miles apart - share the exact same time, Bristol of the early 1800s was approximately ten minutes behind the capital.". Cụm ten minutes khớp với yêu cầu câu hỏi và đúng giới hạn từ nếu đề có quy định.
q14.evidence: You might be surprised to learn that time as we know it - a precise measurement structured into ordered geographic zones of regular intervals - is a relatively recent phenomenon. If you went back as few as two or three centuries, you would find the human experience of time to be quite different, especially if your time travel was followed by travel of the traditional variety. For example, whereas now London and Bristol - approximately 100 miles apart - share the exact same time, Bristol of the early 1800s was approximately ten minutes behind the capital._

q15.explanation: Cần điền/chọn thông tin đúng từ bài đọc. Ở đoạn 3, bài viết nêu: "For the most part, and certainly relative to the Britain of today, the country was rather disconnected. There were, of course, roads connecting all settlements regardless of size, but because journeys between them were slow there was little call for exact measurements of duration. Besides, each little town kept its own time, based on its own astronomical observations - specifically the location of the sun throughout the day - and thus kept their own slow pace.". Cụm exact measurements khớp với yêu cầu câu hỏi và đúng giới hạn từ nếu đề có quy định.
q15.evidence: For the most part, and certainly relative to the Britain of today, the country was rather disconnected. There were, of course, roads connecting all settlements regardless of size, but because journeys between them were slow there was little call for exact measurements of duration. Besides, each little town kept its own time, based on its own astronomical observations - specifically the location of the sun throughout the day - and thus kept their own slow pace._

q17.explanation: Cần điền/chọn thông tin đúng từ bài đọc. Ở đoạn 4, bài viết nêu: "As industrial centres ‘blossomed’ far from London, so the need for improved transport links to facilitate trade became more urgent. With the introduction of railroads, the journey to our familiar ‘small world’ had begun. To assist the increasingly confused travellers, who were forced to re-adjust their watches at every station, a standard British time based upon the yearly average time the sun crossed the Greenwich Meridian was introduced.". Cụm railroads khớp với yêu cầu câu hỏi và đúng giới hạn từ nếu đề có quy định.
q17.evidence: As industrial centres ‘blossomed’ far from London, so the need for improved transport links to facilitate trade became more urgent. With the introduction of railroads, the journey to our familiar ‘small world’ had begun. To assist the increasingly confused travellers, who were forced to re-adjust their watches at every station, a standard British time based upon the yearly average time the sun crossed the Greenwich Meridian was introduced._

q18.explanation: Cần điền/chọn thông tin đúng từ bài đọc. Ở đoạn 5, bài viết nêu: "While Greenwich’s role in establishing the concept of time standardisation was crucial, the diminutive size of the country meant that the problems Britain had faced were solved by this simple development. Across the Atlantic, American infrastructure and industry were expanding so fast and on such scale that further adaptations had to be made. The introduction of 100 railroad time zones, while a step in the right direction, was only a partial solution and as trains became more rapid, so safety concerns become greater - with many trains sharing a single track, exact time became critical.". Cụm size khớp với yêu cầu câu hỏi và đúng giới hạn từ nếu đề có quy định.
q18.evidence: While Greenwich’s role in establishing the concept of time standardisation was crucial, the diminutive size of the country meant that the problems Britain had faced were solved by this simple development. Across the Atlantic, American infrastructure and industry were expanding so fast and on such scale that further adaptations had to be made. The introduction of 100 railroad time zones, while a step in the right direction, was only a partial solution and as trains became more rapid, so safety concerns become greater - with many trains sharing a single track, exact time became critical._

q20.explanation: Cần điền/chọn thông tin đúng từ bài đọc. Ở đoạn 6, bài viết nêu: "Half of the city’s businesses obeyed, but many individuals refused, citing what they saw as the ‘dehumanising effect’ of exact time. Incredibly, this reluctance to standardise influenced the City Council to revert to its original solar time. However, it wasn’t long until pressure from railway companies forced them to re-adapt.". Cụm railway companies khớp với yêu cầu câu hỏi và đúng giới hạn từ nếu đề có quy định.

Incredibly, this reluctance to standardise influenced the City Council to revert to its original solar time. However, it wasn’t long until pressure from railway companies forced them to re-adapt._
